content:
# Deuteronomy 7 — Disagreement Log
**Chapter:** Deuteronomy 7 (26 verses) | **Arbiter:** SOPHIA

---

## V.1 — "greater and more numerous" vs. "larger and more powerful"

- **EMET:** "seven nations greater and more numerous than you"
- **LOGOS:** "seven nations greater and mightier than you"
- **PNEUMA:** "seven nations larger and more powerful than you"
- **Ruling:** "greater and more numerous" — literal to Hebrew rabbim (numerous/many) and atsumim (strong/mighty). LOGOS uses "mightier" which is acceptable; PNEUMA's "more powerful" departs from the numerical sense of rabbim.

---

## V.2 — cherem rendering

- **EMET:** "you shall utterly devote them to destruction [cherem]"
- **LOGOS:** "you shall utterly devote them to destruction"
- **PNEUMA:** "you must place them under the ban of destruction"
- **Ruling:** "you shall utterly devote them to destruction" — PNEUMA's "ban of destruction" is explanatory but unnecessarily paraphrastic. Gloss dropped as cherem convention is established.

---

## V.2 — "show them no favor" vs. "show them no mercy"

- **EMET:** "show them no favor"
- **LOGOS:** "show them no favor"
- **PNEUMA:** "show them no mercy"
- **Ruling:** "show them no favor" — Hebrew lo techonnem (from chanan = to show grace/favor), not racham (mercy). PNEUMA's "mercy" is imprecise.

---

## V.4 — "your son" vs. "your sons" vs. "your children"

- **EMET:** "he would turn your son away"
- **LOGOS:** "they would turn away your sons"
- **PNEUMA:** "they will turn your children away"
- **Ruling:** "he would turn your son away" — MT binkha is singular. LOGOS and PNEUMA normalize to plural. MT singular preserved.

---

## V.5 — "hew down" vs. "cut down"

- **EMET:** "their Asherah-poles you shall hew down"
- **LOGOS:** "cut down their Asherah poles"
- **PNEUMA:** "cut down their Asherah poles"
- **Ruling:** "hew down" — Hebrew tigde'un from gada' implies forceful chopping/hewing. Marginal difference; "hew down" preferred for its more forceful register.

---

## V.6 — am segullah rendering

- **EMET:** "a treasured people [am segullah]"
- **LOGOS:** "a people for his treasured possession [am segullah]"
- **PNEUMA:** "his own treasured possession"
- **Ruling:** "a people of his own treasured possession" — synthesizes the grammatical structure of LOGOS with the relational emphasis of PNEUMA's "own."

---

## V.8 — "strong hand" vs. "mighty hand"

- **EMET:** "with a strong hand"
- **LOGOS:** "with a mighty hand"
- **PNEUMA:** "with a mighty hand"
- **Ruling:** "strong hand" — Hebrew yad chazaqah: chazaq = strong/firm. "Mighty" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is a common rendering but "strong" is more literal.

---

## V.9 — hesed compound rendering

- **EMET:** "keeping the covenant and the covenant-faithfulness [hesed]"
- **LOGOS:** "keeps covenant and covenant-faithfulness [hesed]"
- **PNEUMA:** "keeps his covenant of love"
- **Ruling:** "keeping the covenant and the covenant-faithfulness" — PNEUMA's "covenant of love" blends two distinct terms (berit + hesed) into one. Established convention kept: hesed = "covenant-faithfulness."

---

## V.10 — structure of repayment verse

- **EMET:** "repaying those who hate him to their face, to destroy them; he will not be late with the one who hates him — to his face he will repay him"
- **LOGOS:** "repays to their face those who hate him, by destroying them. He will not delay with the one who hates him; he will repay him to his face"
- **PNEUMA:** "he repays with destruction those who reject him — he will not be slow to punish those who hate him; he repays them to their face"
- **Ruling:** Follows EMET/LOGOS structure. PNEUMA's "those who reject him" loses the Hebrew sone'av (those who hate him). "Will not delay" (LOGOS) preferred to "will not be late" (EMET). "Hate" retained throughout.

---

## V.11 — "ordinances" vs. "rules"

- **EMET:** "the commandment and the statutes and the ordinances"
- **LOGOS:** "the commandment and the statutes and the rules"
- **PNEUMA:** "the commands and statutes and ordinances"
- **Ruling:** "the commandment and the statutes and the ordinances" — Hebrew mitsvah, choq, mishpat. "Ordinances" retained for mishpat (judgments/ordinances); LOGOS's "rules" is less precise.

---

## V.12 — "heed" vs. "listen to" vs. "obey"

- **EMET:** "because you heed these ordinances"
- **LOGOS:** "because you listen to these rules"
- **PNEUMA:** "if you listen to these ordinances"
- **Ruling:** "because you heed" — "heed" captures the Hebrew shama' (to hear/heed) more fully than "listen to" alone.

---

## V.13 — "fresh oil" vs. "oil" vs. "olive oil"

- **EMET:** "your new wine and your fresh oil"
- **LOGOS:** "your wine and your oil"
- **PNEUMA:** "your new wine and your olive oil"
- **Ruling:** "your new wine and your fresh oil" — Hebrew yitshar = fresh/new oil (as opposed to stored oil). LOGOS drops the qualifier; PNEUMA specifies "olive oil" (slightly interpretive). "Fresh oil" is most accurate.

---

## V.14 — "barren male or barren female" vs. variants

- **EMET:** "there shall not be in you a barren male or a barren female"
- **LOGOS:** "there shall not be male or female barren among you"
- **PNEUMA:** "not a man or woman among you will be barren"
- **Ruling:** "there shall not be a barren male or a barren female among you" — EMET's word order preserved; Hebrew 'aqar (barren male) and 'aqarah (barren female) are specific terms.

---

## V.15 — "evil diseases" vs. "terrible sicknesses"

- **EMET:** "all the evil diseases of Egypt"
- **LOGOS:** "none of the evil diseases of Egypt"
- **PNEUMA:** "any of the terrible sicknesses you knew in Egypt"
- **Ruling:** "all the evil diseases of Egypt" — EMET's positive assertion followed by negation is structurally sound. LOGOS restructures. "Evil diseases" preferred to PNEUMA's "terrible sicknesses."

---

## V.16 — "look with pity" vs. "pity them"

- **EMET:** "your eye shall not look with pity upon them"
- **LOGOS:** "Your eye shall not pity them"
- **PNEUMA:** "Do not look on them with pity"
- **Ruling:** "your eye shall not look with pity on them" — EMET preserves the Hebrew idiom 'ein lo tachmos (your eye shall not show compassion). Minor structural variation.

---

## V.17 — "more numerous than I" vs. "greater than I/us"

- **EMET:** "These nations are more numerous than I"
- **LOGOS:** "These nations are greater than I"
- **PNEUMA:** "These nations are greater than us"
- **Ruling:** "These nations are more numerous than I" — Hebrew rabbim mimmenni = "more numerous than I." LOGOS and PNEUMA use "greater" (LOGOS) and "greater than us" (PNEUMA, wrong person). MT preserved.

---

## V.18 — "you shall well remember" vs. "you shall remember" vs. "Remember clearly"

- **EMET:** "you shall well remember"
- **LOGOS:** "you shall remember"
- **PNEUMA:** "Remember clearly"
- **Ruling:** "you shall well remember" — Hebrew zachor tizkor (absolute infinitive + finite verb) = remember, remember / well remember. The intensive construction is preserved.

---

## V.20 — "hornets" detail

- **EMET:** "the hornets [tsir'ah]"
- **LOGOS:** "the hornet"
- **PNEUMA:** "hornets"
- **Ruling:** "the hornets" — plural rendering with definite article. Minor divergence; plural is slightly more natural.

---

## V.21 — "tremble" vs. "be in dread" vs. "be terrified"

- **EMET:** "You shall not tremble before them"
- **LOGOS:** "You shall not be in dread of them"
- **PNEUMA:** "Do not be terrified by them"
- **Ruling:** "You shall not tremble before them" — Hebrew lo ta'arots (from arats = to tremble/be terrified). All renderings are defensible; "tremble" preserves the physical-kinetic sense.

---

## V.22 — "finish them off quickly" vs. "make an end of them at once"

- **EMET:** "you will not be able to finish them off quickly"
- **LOGOS:** "You may not make an end of them at once"
- **PNEUMA:** "You cannot sweep them away all at once"
- **Ruling:** "you may not make an end of them all at once" — LOGOS's phrasing is clearest. EMET's "quickly" is slightly imprecise for maher (sudden/rapid pace; here "all at once" is the better sense contextually).

---

## V.25 — "abomination" vs. "detestable"

- **EMET:** "for it is an abomination [to'evah] to the LORD"
- **LOGOS:** "for it is an abomination to the LORD"
- **PNEUMA:** "for it is detestable to the LORD your God"
- **Ruling:** "abomination" — established convention for to'evah throughout this translation.

---

## V.26 — final verse rendering

- **EMET:** "you shall utterly loathe it and utterly abhor it"
- **LOGOS:** "You shall utterly detest and abhor it"
- **PNEUMA:** "Treat it as utterly revolting and repulsive"
- **Ruling:** "you shall utterly detest it and utterly abhor it" — preserving LOGOS's word choice with EMET's parallel structure. PNEUMA's "revolting and repulsive" is vivid but too paraphrastic for the double Hiphil construction.

---

**Total disagreements logged: 23**

— SOPHIA
